forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KTC7Y50PEM427HQ79NW52WY4
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: 8a80
+project: semantic-search
+title: BM25, trigram-Dice, and RRF scoring primitives for search fusion
+---
+## What
+Add the three pure scoring primitives the fused `search code` op composes. New module `crates/swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/search_fusion/score.rs` (sibling of `tokenize.rs` from the tokenizer card). All functions are pure â€” no DB, no embeddings â€” and consume `super::tokenize`.
+
+Primitives (encode the agreed math exactly; constants must be named):
+- BM25 (Okapi), two halves so the op can do its single corpus pass then per-chunk scoring:
+  - `pub struct Bm25Corpus { /* df per query term, N (doc count), avgdl (mean token count) */ }` plus a builder that, given the query tokens and an iterator of per-document token counts + which query terms each doc contains, computes `df(t)` and `avgdl`. Keep the public shape minimal but testable.
+  - `pub fn bm25_score(corpus: &Bm25Corpus, doc_tokens: &[String], query_tokens: &[String]) -> f32` implementing `Î£_t IDF(t)Â·tfÂ·(k1+1)/(tf + k1Â·(1âˆ’b+bÂ·|D|/avgdl))` with `const K1: f32 = 1.2; const B: f32 = 0.75;` and `IDF(t)=ln(1+(Nâˆ’df+0.5)/(df+0.5))`. `tf` is term frequency of `t` in `doc_tokens`; `|D|` is `doc_tokens.len()`.
+- Trigram-Dice overlap:
+  - `pub fn trigram_dice(query: &str, target: &str) -> f32` = `2Â·|AÃ¢Â©B| / (|A|+|B|)` over char-trigram SETS (use `char_trigrams` from the tokenizer card; dedupe to sets). Returns 0.0 when either side has no trigrams. NOTE: the op will call this against the chunk's `symbol_path` + identifier tokens joined, NOT the whole chunk text â€” that scoping lives in the op card, this primitive just scores two strings.
+- Reciprocal Rank Fusion:
+  - `pub fn rrf_fuse(ranked_lists: &[&[usize]], weights: &[f32], k: f32) -> HashMap<usize, f32>` (or a Vec indexed by doc id) implementing `RRF(d)=Î£_r w_r/(k+rank_r(d))`, `const RRF_K: f32 = 60.0` as the default `k`. Each input list is doc ids in rank order (rank 0 = best). A doc absent from a list contributes nothing for that list. Document the rank base (0 vs 1) and use it consistently.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `bm25_score` matches a hand-computed Okapi value (within 1e-4) for a tiny 3-doc corpus with a 1-term and a 2-term query; rarer terms (lower df) score higher.
+- [ ] `trigram_dice("get_user", "get_user")` == 1.0; `trigram_dice("getUsr", "get_user")` is high (> 0.4) proving the typo-rescue; disjoint strings score 0.0.
+- [ ] `rrf_fuse` ranks a doc that is rank-0 in two of three lists above a doc that is rank-0 in only one; equal weights `[1.0,1.0,1.0]` and `k=60` reproduce a hand-computed fused ordering.
+- [ ] `K1`, `B`, `RRF_K` are named `const`s, not inline literals.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Unit tests in `crates/swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/search_fusion/score.rs` `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`: BM25 IDF/tf math vs hand-computed values, df-monotonicity (rarer term scores higher), Dice identity/typo/disjoint cases, RRF fusion ordering + weight effect + missing-doc handling.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-code-context search_fusion` passes (all new tests green).
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` â€” write failing tests first, then implement to pass.
\ No newline at end of file

Returns 0.0 when either side has no trigrams. NOTE: the op will call this against the chunk's `symbol_path` + identifier tokens joined, NOT the whole chunk text â€” that scoping lives in the op card, this primitive just scores two strings.
-- Reciprocal Rank Fusion:
-  - `pub fn rrf_fuse(ranked_lists: &[&[usize]], weights: &[f32], k: f32) -> HashMap<usize, f32>` (or a Vec indexed by doc id) implementing `RRF(d)=Î£_r w_r/(k+rank_r(d))`, `const RRF_K: f32 = 60.0` as the default `k`. Each input list is doc ids in rank order (rank 0 = best). A doc absent from a list contributes nothing for that list. Document the rank base (0 vs 1) and use it consistently.
-
-## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `bm25_score` matches a hand-computed Okapi value (within 1e-4) for a tiny 3-doc corpus with a 1-term and a 2-term query; rarer terms (lower df) score higher.

-- [ ] `rrf_fuse` ranks a doc that is rank-0 in two of three lists above a doc that is rank-0 in only one; equal weights `[1.0,1.0,1.0]` and `k=60` reproduce a hand-computed fused ordering.

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -6,29 +6,44 @@
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: 8a80
 project: semantic-search
-title: BM25, trigram-Dice, and RRF scoring primitives for search fusion
+title: 'swissarmyhammer-search: BM25 + trigram-Dice + RRF + cosine + search()'
 ---
 ## What
-Add the three pure scoring primitives the fused `search code` op composes. New module `crates/swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/search_fusion/score.rs` (sibling of `tokenize.rs` from the tokenizer card). All functions are pure â€” no DB, no embeddings â€” and consume `super::tokenize`.
+Implement the scoring primitives and the top-level `search()` in the new `swissarmyhammer-search` crate (created in the types+tokenizer card). All pure, in-memory, NO DB, NO FTS5, NO ANN, NO persistence.

-Primitives (encode the agreed math exactly; constants must be named):
-- BM25 (Okapi), two halves so the op can do its single corpus pass then per-chunk scoring:
-  - `pub struct Bm25Corpus { /* df per query term, N (doc count), avgdl (mean token count) */ }` plus a builder that, given the query tokens and an iterator of per-document token counts + which query terms each doc contains, computes `df(t)` and `avgdl`. Keep the public shape minimal but testable.
-  - `pub fn bm25_score(corpus: &Bm25Corpus, doc_tokens: &[String], query_tokens: &[String]) -> f32` implementing `Î£_t IDF(t)Â·tfÂ·(k1+1)/(tf + k1Â·(1âˆ’b+bÂ·|D|/avgdl))` with `const K1: f32 = 1.2; const B: f32 = 0.75;` and `IDF(t)=ln(1+(Nâˆ’df+0.5)/(df+0.5))`. `tf` is term frequency of `t` in `doc_tokens`; `|D|` is `doc_tokens.len()`.
-- Trigram-Dice overlap:
-  - `pub fn trigram_dice(query: &str, target: &str) -> f32` = `2Â·|AÃ¢Â©B| / (|A|+|B|)` over char-trigram SETS (use `char_trigrams` from the tokenizer card; dedupe to sets). Returns 0.0 when either side has no trigrams. NOTE: the op will call this against the chunk's `symbol_path` + identifier tokens joined, NOT the whole chunk text â€” that scoping lives in the op card, this primitive just scores two strings.
-- Reciprocal Rank Fusion:
-  - `pub fn rrf_fuse(ranked_lists: &[&[usize]], weights: &[f32], k: f32) -> HashMap<usize, f32>` (or a Vec indexed by doc id) implementing `RRF(d)=Î£_r w_r/(k+rank_r(d))`, `const RRF_K: f32 = 60.0` as the default `k`. Each input list is doc ids in rank order (rank 0 = best). A doc absent from a list contributes nothing for that list. Document the rank base (0 vs 1) and use it consistently.
+Files to create/edit:
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-search/src/score.rs` â€” BM25, trigram-Dice, RRF primitives (consume `crate::tokenize`).
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-search/src/cosine.rs` â€” inlined `cosine_similarity` + little-endian f32 blob helpers (do NOT depend on `model_embedding`).
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-search/src/lib.rs` â€” add `pub fn search(docs: &[Doc], q: &Query) -> Vec<Hit>` (the two-pass loop) and re-export the helpers.

+Scoring primitives (named constants, exact math):
+- BM25 (Okapi), two halves so `search()` does one corpus pass then per-doc scoring:
+  - `pub struct Bm25Corpus { /* df per query term, N (doc count), avgdl (mean token count) */ }` + a builder taking query tokens and an iterator of per-doc token counts + which query terms each doc contains; computes `df(t)` and `avgdl`.
+  - `pub fn bm25_score(corpus: &Bm25Corpus, doc_tokens: &[String], query_tokens: &[String]) -> f32` implementing `Î£_t IDF(t)Â·tfÂ·(k1+1)/(tf + k1Â·(1âˆ’b+bÂ·|D|/avgdl))` with `const K1: f32 = 1.2; const B: f32 = 0.75;` and `IDF(t)=ln(1+(Nâˆ’df+0.5)/(df+0.5))`. `tf` = term frequency of `t` in `doc_tokens`; `|D|` = `doc_tokens.len()`.
+- Trigram-Dice: `pub fn trigram_dice(query: &str, target: &str) -> f32` = `2Â·|Aâˆ©B| / (|A|+|B|)` over char-trigram SETS (use `char_trigrams`; dedupe to sets). Returns 0.0 when either side has no trigrams.
+- RRF: `pub fn rrf_fuse(ranked_lists: &[&[usize]], weights: &[f32], k: f32) -> HashMap<usize, f32>` implementing `RRF(d)=Î£_r w_r/(k+rank_r(d))`, `const RRF_K: f32 = 60.0` default `k`. Each input list is doc indices in rank order (rank 0 = best). A doc absent from a list contributes nothing for that list. Document the rank base (0) and use it consistently.
+
+Cosine + blob helpers (`cosine.rs`):
+- `pub fn cosine_similarity(a: &[f32], b: &[f32]) -> f32` â€” dot/norm; returns 0.0 for empty or mismatched-length inputs (match the semantics the old `model_embedding::cosine_similarity` had â€” see the existing code-context unit tests for the contract: identical=1.0, orthogonal=0.0, opposite=-1.0, empty=0.0, mismatched=0.0).
+- `pub fn serialize_embedding(embedding: &[f32]) -> Vec<u8>` (little-endian f32 blob) and `pub fn deserialize_embedding(blob: &[u8]) -> Vec<f32>` (these mirror the helpers currently inline in `code-context/src/ops/search_code.rs`; callers persist vectors).
+
+Top-level `search()` (two passes over the in-memory `&[Doc]`):
+- Pass 1: tokenize `q.text` once; build `Bm25Corpus` (df per query term, avgdl over all docs).
+- Pass 2 per doc: compute `bm25` over the doc's field texts (with `Field.weight` multiplying that field's lexical contribution â€” title/symbol_path outrank body); `trigram` over the high-weight fields/identifier tokens; `cosine` over `q.embedding` vs `doc.embedding` when BOTH are present.
+- Fuse: rank docs within each PRESENT signal list, `rrf_fuse` with `q.weights`. A signal with no data (e.g. all `embedding: None`, or `q.embedding: None`) is simply absent from fusion â€” graceful degradation, not a zero-fill. Sort by fused score desc, apply `q.min_score` floor if set, take `q.top_k`. Return `Vec<Hit>` carrying the per-signal `Signals` for debugging rank order.
+
 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] `bm25_score` matches a hand-computed Okapi value (within 1e-4) for a tiny 3-doc corpus with a 1-term and a 2-term query; rarer terms (lower df) score higher.
-- [ ] `trigram_dice("get_user", "get_user")` == 1.0; `trigram_dice("getUsr", "get_user")` is high (> 0.4) proving the typo-rescue; disjoint strings score 0.0.
-- [ ] `rrf_fuse` ranks a doc that is rank-0 in two of three lists above a doc that is rank-0 in only one; equal weights `[1.0,1.0,1.0]` and `k=60` reproduce a hand-computed fused ordering.
+- [ ] `trigram_dice("get_user","get_user")` == 1.0; `trigram_dice("getUsr","get_user")` > 0.4 (typo-rescue); disjoint strings score 0.0.
+- [ ] `rrf_fuse` ranks a doc rank-0 in two of three lists above a doc rank-0 in only one; equal weights `[1,1,1]`, `k=60` reproduce a hand-computed fused ordering.
+- [ ] `cosine_similarity` satisfies identical=1.0, orthogonal=0.0, opposite=-1.0, empty=0.0, mismatched-length=0.0.
+- [ ] `serialize_embedding`/`deserialize_embedding` round-trip a `Vec<f32>` exactly.
+- [ ] `search()` ranks a doc with a strong high-weight-field lexical match but weak cosine above a doc with mediocre signals; when no doc has an embedding the cosine signal is absent (not zero-filled) and results still come back.
 - [ ] `K1`, `B`, `RRF_K` are named `const`s, not inline literals.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Unit tests in `crates/swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/ops/search_fusion/score.rs` `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`: BM25 IDF/tf math vs hand-computed values, df-monotonicity (rarer term scores higher), Dice identity/typo/disjoint cases, RRF fusion ordering + weight effect + missing-doc handling.
-- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-code-context search_fusion` passes (all new tests green).
+- [ ] Unit tests in `score.rs`, `cosine.rs`, and a `search()` test module: BM25 IDF/tf vs hand-computed values + df-monotonicity; Dice identity/typo/disjoint; RRF ordering + weight effect + missing-doc handling; cosine contract cases + blob round-trip; `search()` fusion-ordering + graceful-degradation (embedding: None) cases.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-search` passes (all new tests green).

 ## Workflow
 - Use `/tdd` â€” write failing tests first, then implement to pass.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -16,33 +16,46 @@
 - `crates/swissarmyhammer-search/src/cosine.rs` â€” inlined `cosine_similarity` + little-endian f32 blob helpers (do NOT depend on `model_embedding`).

+### Field weighting model (DECIDED â€” weighted-tf BM25F-lite)
+A doc has multiple `Field`s with weights. Combine them as **weighted term frequency under a single global IDF** (NOT per-field BM25 summed, NOT full BM25F with per-field length norm):
+- One IDF per term, computed over the corpus where `df(t)` = number of docs containing `t` in ANY field.
+- When scoring a doc, `tf(t, doc)` = sum of `Field.weight` over each occurrence of `t` across all the doc's fields (a term appearing once in a weight-3.0 title contributes `tf += 3.0`; once in a weight-1.0 body contributes `tf += 1.0`).
+- `|D|` (length normalization) = the doc's UNWEIGHTED total token count; `avgdl` = mean unweighted token count across all docs.
+This makes title/symbol_path matches naturally outrank body matches with a single IDF and one pass, and is hand-computable for tests.
+
 Scoring primitives (named constants, exact math):
 - BM25 (Okapi), two halves so `search()` does one corpus pass then per-doc scoring:
-  - `pub struct Bm25Corpus { /* df per query term, N (doc count), avgdl (mean token count) */ }` + a builder taking query tokens and an iterator of per-doc token counts + which query terms each doc contains; computes `df(t)` and `avgdl`.
-  - `pub fn bm25_score(corpus: &Bm25Corpus, doc_tokens: &[String], query_tokens: &[String]) -> f32` implementing `Î£_t IDF(t)Â·tfÂ·(k1+1)/(tf + k1Â·(1âˆ’b+bÂ·|D|/avgdl))` with `const K1: f32 = 1.2; const B: f32 = 0.75;` and `IDF(t)=ln(1+(Nâˆ’df+0.5)/(df+0.5))`. `tf` = term frequency of `t` in `doc_tokens`; `|D|` = `doc_tokens.len()`.
-- Trigram-Dice: `pub fn trigram_dice(query: &str, target: &str) -> f32` = `2Â·|Aâˆ©B| / (|A|+|B|)` over char-trigram SETS (use `char_trigrams`; dedupe to sets). Returns 0.0 when either side has no trigrams.
-- RRF: `pub fn rrf_fuse(ranked_lists: &[&[usize]], weights: &[f32], k: f32) -> HashMap<usize, f32>` implementing `RRF(d)=Î£_r w_r/(k+rank_r(d))`, `const RRF_K: f32 = 60.0` default `k`. Each input list is doc indices in rank order (rank 0 = best). A doc absent from a list contributes nothing for that list. Document the rank base (0) and use it consistently.
+  - `pub struct Bm25Corpus { /* df per query term, N (doc count), avgdl (mean UNWEIGHTED token count) */ }` + a builder taking query tokens and an iterator of per-doc unweighted token counts + which query terms each doc contains (in any field); computes `df(t)` and `avgdl`.
+  - `pub fn bm25_score(corpus: &Bm25Corpus, weighted_tf: &HashMap<&str, f32>, doc_len: usize, query_tokens: &[String]) -> f32` implementing `Î£_t IDF(t)Â·tfÂ·(k1+1)/(tf + k1Â·(1âˆ’b+bÂ·|D|/avgdl))` with `const K1: f32 = 1.2; const B: f32 = 0.75;` and `IDF(t)=ln(1+(Nâˆ’df+0.5)/(df+0.5))`. `tf` = the weighted term frequency described above; `|D|` = `doc_len` (unweighted).
+- Trigram-Dice: `pub fn trigram_dice(query: &str, target: &str) -> f32` = `2Â·|Aâˆ©B| / (|A|+|B|)` over char-trigram SETS (use `char_trigrams`; dedupe to sets). Returns 0.0 when either side has no trigrams. The per-doc trigram SIGNAL (in `search()`) is the weighted sum across the doc's fields: `Î£_field weight_field Â· trigram_dice(q.text, field.text)` â€” no magic "high-weight" threshold; high-weight fields dominate. (Absolute scale is irrelevant: RRF consumes only the rank.)
+- RRF: `pub fn rrf_fuse(ranked_lists: &[&[usize]], weights: &[f32], k: f32) -> HashMap<usize, f32>` implementing `RRF(d)=Î£_r w_r/(k+rank_r(d))`, `const RRF_K: f32 = 60.0` default `k`. Each input list is doc indices in rank order (rank 0 = best). A doc absent from a list contributes nothing for that list. Ranking within a signal uses a STABLE sort (ties keep input/doc-index order) so fused rankings are deterministic in tests. Document the rank base (0).

+### Fused-score normalization (DECIDED â€” surface an interpretable score)
+Raw RRF scores are tiny/unintuitive (three signals at rank 0, k=60 â†’ â‰ˆ0.05). Normalize the fused score to [0,1] by dividing by the max achievable for the PRESENT signals: `max = Î£_{present r} w_r / (k + 0)`. This is monotone (ranking unchanged) but makes the surfaced `Hit.score` and the `Query.min_score` floor meaningful. `min_score` is applied to this NORMALIZED score.
+
 Cosine + blob helpers (`cosine.rs`):
-- `pub fn cosine_similarity(a: &[f32], b: &[f32]) -> f32` â€” dot/norm; returns 0.0 for empty or mismatched-length inputs (match the semantics the old `model_embedding::cosine_similarity` had â€” see the existing code-context unit tests for the contract: identical=1.0, orthogonal=0.0, opposite=-1.0, empty=0.0, mismatched=0.0).
-- `pub fn serialize_embedding(embedding: &[f32]) -> Vec<u8>` (little-endian f32 blob) and `pub fn deserialize_embedding(blob: &[u8]) -> Vec<f32>` (these mirror the helpers currently inline in `code-context/src/ops/search_code.rs`; callers persist vectors).
+- `pub fn cosine_similarity(a: &[f32], b: &[f32]) -> f32` â€” dot/norm; returns 0.0 for empty or mismatched-length inputs (match the semantics the old `model_embedding::cosine_similarity` had â€” identical=1.0, orthogonal=0.0, opposite=-1.0, empty=0.0, mismatched=0.0).
+- `pub fn serialize_embedding(embedding: &[f32]) -> Vec<u8>` (little-endian f32 blob) and `pub fn deserialize_embedding(blob: &[u8]) -> Vec<f32>` (mirror the helpers currently inline in `code-context/src/ops/search_code.rs`; callers persist vectors).

 Top-level `search()` (two passes over the in-memory `&[Doc]`):
-- Pass 1: tokenize `q.text` once; build `Bm25Corpus` (df per query term, avgdl over all docs).
-- Pass 2 per doc: compute `bm25` over the doc's field texts (with `Field.weight` multiplying that field's lexical contribution â€” title/symbol_path outrank body); `trigram` over the high-weight fields/identifier tokens; `cosine` over `q.embedding` vs `doc.embedding` when BOTH are present.
-- Fuse: rank docs within each PRESENT signal list, `rrf_fuse` with `q.weights`. A signal with no data (e.g. all `embedding: None`, or `q.embedding: None`) is simply absent from fusion â€” graceful degradation, not a zero-fill. Sort by fused score desc, apply `q.min_score` floor if set, take `q.top_k`. Return `Vec<Hit>` carrying the per-signal `Signals` for debugging rank order.
+- Tokenize each doc's fields ONCE (reuse across stats + scoring â€” do not re-tokenize per pass). Tokenize `q.text` once.
+- Pass 1: build `Bm25Corpus` (df per query term over docs containing the term in any field; avgdl over unweighted doc token counts).
+- Pass 2 per doc: compute `bm25` (weighted-tf BM25F-lite above); `trigram` (weighted-sum Dice above); `cosine` over `q.embedding` vs `doc.embedding` when BOTH are present.
+- Fuse: rank docs within each PRESENT signal list, `rrf_fuse` with `q.weights`. A signal with no data (e.g. all `embedding: None`, or `q.embedding: None`, or empty `q.text`) is simply absent from fusion â€” graceful degradation, not a zero-fill. Normalize fused score to [0,1] (above). Sort by fused score desc, apply `q.min_score` floor if set, take `q.top_k`. Return `Vec<Hit>` carrying the per-signal `Signals` (raw bm25/trigram/cosine values, for debugging rank order).

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] `bm25_score` matches a hand-computed Okapi value (within 1e-4) for a tiny 3-doc corpus with a 1-term and a 2-term query; rarer terms (lower df) score higher.
+- [ ] Weighted-tf field weighting: a query term present once in a high-weight field scores higher than the same term once in a low-weight field, all else equal (hand-computed).
 - [ ] `trigram_dice("get_user","get_user")` == 1.0; `trigram_dice("getUsr","get_user")` > 0.4 (typo-rescue); disjoint strings score 0.0.
-- [ ] `rrf_fuse` ranks a doc rank-0 in two of three lists above a doc rank-0 in only one; equal weights `[1,1,1]`, `k=60` reproduce a hand-computed fused ordering.
+- [ ] `rrf_fuse` ranks a doc rank-0 in two of three lists above a doc rank-0 in only one; equal weights `[1,1,1]`, `k=60` reproduce a hand-computed fused ordering; ties resolve by input order (stable).
+- [ ] Fused score is normalized to [0,1]: a doc ranked rank-0 in every present signal scores 1.0; normalization does not change ordering.
 - [ ] `cosine_similarity` satisfies identical=1.0, orthogonal=0.0, opposite=-1.0, empty=0.0, mismatched-length=0.0.
 - [ ] `serialize_embedding`/`deserialize_embedding` round-trip a `Vec<f32>` exactly.
-- [ ] `search()` ranks a doc with a strong high-weight-field lexical match but weak cosine above a doc with mediocre signals; when no doc has an embedding the cosine signal is absent (not zero-filled) and results still come back.
+- [ ] `search()` ranks a doc with a strong high-weight-field lexical match but weak cosine above a doc with mediocre signals; when no doc has an embedding the cosine signal is absent (not zero-filled) and results still come back; `min_score` filters on the normalized score.
 - [ ] `K1`, `B`, `RRF_K` are named `const`s, not inline literals.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Unit tests in `score.rs`, `cosine.rs`, and a `search()` test module: BM25 IDF/tf vs hand-computed values + df-monotonicity; Dice identity/typo/disjoint; RRF ordering + weight effect + missing-doc handling; cosine contract cases + blob round-trip; `search()` fusion-ordering + graceful-degradation (embedding: None) cases.
+- [ ] Unit tests in `score.rs`, `cosine.rs`, and a `search()` test module: BM25 IDF/tf vs hand-computed values + df-monotonicity + weighted-tf field effect; Dice identity/typo/disjoint; RRF ordering + weight effect + stable ties + missing-doc handling; fused-score normalization (rank-0-everywhere == 1.0, ordering preserved); cosine contract cases + blob round-trip; `search()` fusion-ordering + graceful-degradation (embedding: None / empty query) + min_score-on-normalized.
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-search` passes (all new tests green).
